metaTitle: The Boeing Atmosphere Global Documentation Hub - Design tokens

header.title: Design tokens
header.description: Design tokens are reusable building blocks that store design system values, enabling consistent and scalable UI development

sections.text:
What are tokens?

Design tokens store all the values needed to construct and maintain a design system.

Examples: Color, type, opacity, shadows, corner radius.

On the top right, a hex code value represents color. By turning it into a token called "flight-60," it becomes integrated in the design system.

Why use tokens?

Faster hand-off process

If designers and developers both change to this system of tools that speak to each other, there is greater alignment.

Scale with less doubt

Issues that come up can be handled by the design system's team, technology, and established practices.

Saves time with reuseable work

Tokens help standardize decisions across a design system, allowing for flexibility and standardized decisions across design and development.

More engineering time

Reduced iterations from design QA means engineers can spend less time perfecting UI and work on other things.

Faster product changes

Tokens reduce UI development work among engineers. They just need to approve it on Storybook.

Higher quality UI/UX

A designer's work can be directly implemented at the quality they want without them having to learn code.

Consistency between design and development

Tokens ensure consistency as designs are modified, including updates to color modes, themes, densities and dimensions.

Token Types

Base tokens

Base tokens are the lowest building blocks of the design system, and are inherited by all other token types.

In this case, the base token is called "flight-60" and contains the hex code "#009AD7" to represent color.

Alias tokens

Alias tokens communicate the purpose. In this case, the alias token "interactive/primary/background/default" communicates the purpose of how the teal can be used.

The same base token (ex. flight-60) can have multiple alias tokens as instances to communicate different use cases.

items: Atmosphere uses four types of tokens: fonts, colors, dimensions, and effects
Make sure to design with a token's intended purpose, referenced in it's name
The Atmosphere team manages the token set using in the Foundations Figma file
Tokens Studio in Figma acts as the source of truth between design and code

sections.text:
Creating new tokens

Contact the Atmosphere team

The Atmosphere design team is responsible for maintaining design tokens. If you think there is a need or use case for a new token, contact the Atmosphere team and submit a request.

eview current token structure

Identify the core design elements like colors, fonts, spacing, and potentially even things like shadows or animation properties. This helps you understand what tokens you need to create.

Establish a naming convention

Assign clear and descriptive names based on their purpose (e.g., "primary-color" or "button-border-radius"). Consider including different variations or states for elements that have them (e.g., "primary-color-hover").

Add to Tokens Studio

The Tokens Studio plugin is our single source of truth for design tokens. This resource is for both designers and developers. Once the new token has been added, push the changes to GitHub./p>

Add to Figma and publish

Once the token has been added to Tokens Studio, the token must be added to the Figma Foundations Library. In Tokens Studio, select "sync variables". This will update the variables in the Figma file. Next, publish the library with the new variable change.

Document in foundations library

Add to the current design token documentation in the Figma Foundation Library. Include information that explains the token's name, values, and usage guidelines. This helps ensure designers understand how to implement the token correctly.

Best Practices

When creating new tokens, make sure to use lowercase letters within the name. Dots should be used to separate each category. When creating a new style in Figma, slashes should be used in place of dots.

Develop

Integration

New tokens that have been synced to GitLab enter the codebase via the Atmosphere Global Assets Token Service.

Libraries and frameworks

The design tokens are stored in GitLab. They are translated for designers in Tokens Studio. If a change is made, make sure to confirm the change has occurred in Tokens Studio and Figma Foundations file.

Documentation

Rely on the design system documentation to understand the naming conventions, structure, and usage guidelines for the design tokens.

Report a bug

If you encounter a potential issue with tokens through either design or development, report a bug and our team will get back to you as soon as possible.
